threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check your phone for Tube timings instead of just walking to the next bus stop. It's how we all get around these parts.
Last week, I saw a guy in a full penguin suit trying to haggle with the driver over fares at 2 AM. Only in Brixton, right?

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a flock of starlings swoop around the old bakery, just before the morning rush. That chaotic beauty, pure Neukölln.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find spontaneous parties in abandoned lots, with a mix of techno and traditional Turkish music going till dawn. Pure Berlin!

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from here if they can spot a tourist by their confused look at the BART delays, especially during rush hour.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo gently escorting a pigeon off the 47 bus today. You can’t make this city up.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just a party hub, but many locals cherish the quiet evenings in the lesser-known squares, sipping vermut among friends.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their fresh fascination with the Mercat de la Llibertat—locals just grab a quick bite, but they’re there snapping photos of everything.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the TfL app for delays during Tube strikes—locals know to just bike or grab a bus instead.
Caught the 59 last night—young lad serenading everyone with a ukulele. Best part? A lost cat joined in, confused but totally vibing.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I found a random cat on my balcony, purring like it owned the place. Only in Neukölln would a cat seem so at home.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: You can find spontaneous techno parties in hidden basements around here, with no signs. Just a simple flyer in a café if you know where to look.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know they’re local when they know the exact route to avoid the BART chaos after a loud bang at Civic Center. Tourists just stand there confused.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy in a full tuxedo getting ready for a wedding while waiting for the bus at Civic Center. Classic San Francisco moment amidst the chaos.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think we all drink vermut at noon. Most locals prefer a quiet café and a late afternoon caña instead. It's about the vibe, not the clock.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot a recent arrival by their bewilderment at how many local bars serve vermut, thinking it's just a fancy drink when it's a ritual here.
